lens.included_activities: Independent US folding-paperboard-box manufacturers in the approximately $1-10 million normalized EBITDA band that repeatedly design, print, cut, fold, glue, and supply cartons to external customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive packaging operations, paperboard mills, setup-box producers, brokers without accountable production, shells, financing vehicles, and firms outside the EBITDA band.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring business-to-business carton programs for food, beverage, pharmaceutical, personal-care, and other consumer goods, sold through quotes, purchase orders, releases, and supply agreements with charges influenced by board, printing complexity, tooling, run length, and freight.

a.bridge: BLS reports occupations for all paper manufacturing, not wage-weighted tasks in 322212. The production-heavy mix was adjusted upward for folding-carton estimating, structural and graphic prepress support, specification intake, artwork and copy checks, scheduling, purchasing, quality documentation, and customer service; this can overstate exposure where CAD, prepress, and ERP steps are already automated.
a.rationale: Core printing, cutting, folding, gluing, material handling, maintenance, and inspection remain physical. However, folding-carton programs create recurring information work around artwork versions, dielines, regulatory copy, estimates, jobs, approvals, and service that current AI can partly substitute or scale.
a.change_evidence: A payroll-weighted task inventory from independent folding-carton plants, including baseline ERP, CAD, prepress, vision-inspection, and workflow automation, with manual hours by estimating, design, artwork, scheduling, quality, sales, and production.
caveats: Food-contact, pharmaceutical, and brand-critical work requires review even when AI prepares drafts or checks.

rho.rationale: Specification extraction, quote preparation, artwork comparison, version control, schedule assistance, procurement, maintenance retrieval, and service triage are deployable within five years. ERP/prepress integration, customer approval loops, color and print tolerances, food-contact controls, hallucination risk, and accountability keep implementation below the technical ceiling.
rho.change_evidence: Audited carton-plant deployments with before-and-after labor hours, quote cycle time, artwork-error escapes, spoilage, on-time delivery, customer complaints, and required human-review rates.
caveats: No representative five-year implementation study was found.
AI assistance may increase review throughput without reducing payroll proportionally.

e.rationale: Most independent carton converters repeatedly serve external brands and contract packagers and therefore fit the operating lens. Captive facilities, integrated divisions, highly customer-dependent plants, weak stand-alone management, equipment obsolescence, and size-band misclassification remove part of the population.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level map of the 462 employer establishments and their parent firms, marking captive status, external revenue share, normalized EBITDA, customer concentration, equipment condition, certifications, and stand-alone management.
caveats: Census reports establishments, not eligible firms or EBITDA.
The injected n=181 is an ESTIMATE based on size classes and a margin bridge.

s5.bridge: The Census age distribution covers responding owners of employer businesses across all industries, not control sales of folding-carton manufacturers. A fraction of the reported 51% age-55-plus population plus ordinary strategic transactions is converted into a five-year sale probability after excluding internal succession and closure; consolidation can cause an underestimate, while durable family ownership can cause an overestimate.
s5.rationale: Older ownership supports succession pressure, and recurring branded programs plus tangible converting assets can support transfer. Customer approvals, concentration, equipment investment, and family succession mean many potential events will not become qualifying control sales.
s5.change_evidence: Ten years of closed, failed, and withdrawn sale processes for independent US folding-carton firms in the band, combined with current owner-age, succession-plan, and customer-consent interviews.
caveats: Owner-age evidence is all-industry and data year 2018.
Owner age does not measure willingness, sale readiness, or transferability.

q.rationale: Converters can retain benefits embedded in faster launches, fewer artwork errors, reduced spoilage, shorter changeovers, improved reliability, and better service. Board adjustments, competitive rebids, annual cost-down requests, and large consumer-goods customers' purchasing leverage gradually share recurring cost savings.
q.change_evidence: A sample of customer contracts, bid histories, board escalators, annual cost-down provisions, program renewal dates, and margin cohorts before and after measured productivity improvements.
caveats: No representative contract dataset was available.
Retention is likely higher for complex short runs and regulated cartons than for long-run commodity cartons.

d5.bridge: PPC's 1.3% annual folding-carton demand forecast ended in 2026 and is not a 2026-2031 forecast; BLS projects real output for all converted paper products, not 322212 quantity. Applying moderated growth to the current five-year horizon may overstate demand if packaging reduction or flexible alternatives accelerate and understate paper-for-plastic substitution.
d5.rationale: PPC forecast US folding-carton demand growing 1.3% annually to 5.4 million tons by 2026. BLS projects 1.7% annual real-output growth for converted paper products through 2034, while AF&PA says 2025 boxboard production was essentially flat at 12.4 million tons. Together they support modest rather than rapid growth.
d5.change_evidence: The current PPC/Fastmarkets grade- and end-market forecast through 2031, plus quarterly US carton shipments, customer packaged-goods unit volumes, plastic-substitution wins, lightweighting, and capacity closures.
caveats: The direct PPC forecast is stale for the requested horizon.
BLS covers all converted paper products and AF&PA boxboard is an upstream input, not carton demand.

o.rationale: Folding cartons are physical, branded containers requiring accountable manufacture, print and color control, food-contact or product-compliance controls where relevant, quality release, and delivery. Software can mediate orders and automate information work but cannot provide the physical carton without an operator.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of brands self-producing folding cartons at scale, commercially autonomous plants without accountable operating management, or large substitution from cartons to software-only or self-service alternatives.
caveats: Some prototyping and very short runs may move to in-house digital equipment.
Material substitution and package elimination are quantity effects captured in d5.

narrative.executive_view: Folding-carton manufacturers combine recurring external programs and a sizable estimated LMM population with information-rich prepress, artwork, estimating, scheduling, and quality workflows. The core operation remains physical, and the low injected labor-to-receipts ratio caps the economic importance of labor automation.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can extract specifications, prepare estimates, compare artwork revisions, flag copy inconsistencies, assist dieline and schedule choices, draft quality records, retrieve maintenance knowledge, and triage customer requests. Printing, die cutting, folding, gluing, setup, material handling, maintenance, inspection, and accountable approval remain human- or equipment-led.
narrative.value_capture: Speed-to-quote, launch reliability, fewer errors, spoilage reduction, and service differentiation are more retainable than transparent labor cuts. Large brands and contract packagers still use rebids, board escalators, and cost-down negotiations to reclaim a portion.
narrative.firm_availability: Census reports 462 employer establishments, while the frozen margin bridge estimates 181 firms in the EBITDA band. Parent-company integration, captive status, customer concentration, equipment condition, and certifications must be resolved before treating that estimate as a transferable pool.
narrative.demand_durability: Cartons serve recurring food, beverage, medicine, personal-care, and consumer-goods demand. Prior PPC forecasts and current BLS converted-paper output projections indicate modest growth, with paper-for-plastic substitution as an upside and packaging reduction, flexible formats, and weak goods volumes as offsets.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest gaps are the six-digit task mix, current workflow automation, true firm rather than establishment count, margin-band classification, customer concentration, pricing terms, current carton shipment forecasts, and regulatory review burdens. Brand or compliance errors can erase labor savings quickly.
narrative.principal_driver: Recurring, version-heavy carton programs create automatable estimating, artwork, scheduling, quality-documentation, and service work.
narrative.principal_weakness: Low labor intensity and strict brand, food-contact, and quality accountability limit the share of enterprise economics AI can change.

sources.title: Paper Manufacturing: NAICS 322
sources.supports: BLS reports 2025 employment of 87,050 paper-goods machine operators, 17,540 production supervisors, 14,210 industrial-truck operators, and 6,640 production managers in paper manufacturing, supporting a production-heavy occupation mix.

sources.title: Employment and output by industry, 2024-2034
sources.supports: BLS projects converted paper product manufacturing real output from $99.0 billion in 2024 to $116.9 billion in 2034, a 1.7% annual rate, while employment falls 5.4%.

sources.title: 322212: Folding Paperboard Box Manufacturing - Census Bureau Profile
sources.supports: Census defines 322212 as establishments converting non-corrugated paperboard into folding paperboard boxes and reports 462 employer establishments in 2023.

sources.title: Determining the Regulatory Status of Components of a Food Contact Material
sources.supports: FDA states that the overall regulatory status of food-contact material depends on each substance and identifies 21 CFR 176 for paper and paperboard components, supporting compliance and review constraints.

sources.title: AF&PA Releases 66th Annual Paper Industry Capacity and Fiber Consumption Survey
sources.supports: AF&PA reports 2025 boxboard production was essentially flat at 12.4 million tons and overall US paper and paperboard production declined 3.7%; survey data represent about 87% of US capacity with estimates completing the dataset.

sources.title: Business Owners' Ages
sources.supports: The 2019 Annual Business Survey infographic reports 51% of responding employer-business owners were age 55 or older; estimates cover 4.1 million responding owners in data year 2018.

sources.title: Folding Carton Market to Enjoy Measured Growth Through 2026
sources.supports: Paperboard Packaging Council reported a forecast for US folding-carton demand to grow 1.3% annually and reach 5.4 million tons by 2026, with nondurable-goods output forecast to rise 0.8% over that five-year forecast.
